programs.name: Illinois Medicaid (AABD Medical for seniors/disabled)
programs.what_it_does: Provides healthcare coverage including long-term medical and personal care for low-income seniors and disabled individuals. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescription drugs, and personal care services.[2][3][6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors age 65+, disabled individuals, and blind persons meeting income and asset limits.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive health coverage with low or no cost-sharing; includes long-term care services.

programs.name: Home Services Program (HSP) Waiver / Home and Community-Based Services Waiver for the Elderly
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services as an alternative to nursing home care. Includes personal care assistance, home-delivered meals, adult day services, and transportation.[2][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 60+ who are at risk of nursing home placement and meet financial eligibility.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Waiver services to support aging in place, preventing institutionalization.

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to eligible frail elderly through adult day health centers. Integrates Medicare and Medicaid benefits for coordinated care.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors age 55+ certified for nursing home level care who can live safely in the community.[2]
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care covering primary, acute, long-term, and social services.

programs.name: Benefit Access Program (includes QMB, SLMB, QI Medicare Savings Programs)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing for eligible low-income seniors. Helps access Medicare benefits without financial burden.[2][6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors eligible for Medicare meeting specific income thresholds for QMB, SLMB, or QI.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Covers Part A/B premiums (up to $185/month for QMB) and co-pays; income limits vary (e.g., 100-135% FPL).

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides funds via EBT card to purchase food for low-income households. Administered by Illinois Department of Human Services for seniors.[2][3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors age 60+; higher income disregard for elderly/disabled.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-300/month per eligible senior household; no elderly-specific fixed amount.

programs.name: Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers financial assistance for heating/cooling bills and crisis help. Helps low-income households, including seniors, manage energy costs.[2][3]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors at or below 200% FPL; priority for elderly, disabled.[3]
programs.benefit_value: Grants $200-$1,500 per season based on income, household size, fuel type.

programs.name: Illinois Home Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides home energy efficiency improvements like insulation and furnace repairs. Reduces energy costs and improves safety for low-income homes.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors, disabled, and families; priority for vulnerable elderly.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free weatherization services valued at $5,000-$10,000 per home.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare, supplements, and prescription drug plans. Helps seniors select and understand insurance options.[2]
programs.who_its_for: All Illinois Medicare beneficiaries age 65+ or under 65 with disabilities.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; saves thousands in improper coverage choices.

programs.name: Community Care Program (CCP) Home Delivered Meals
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote independence. Often linked to Meals on Wheels providers through aging network.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors age 60+ assessed as needing meal services via CCP.[3]

programs.name: Illinois Caregiver Support Program (includes respite)
programs.what_it_does: Provides support services like respite care, training, and counseling to family caregivers. Offers temporary relief and resources for caregivers of older adults.[2][3]
programs.who_its_for: Unpaid family caregivers of adults age 60+; caregiver age 55+ for some services.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Respite grants up to $2,000/year; training and counseling at no cost.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service work and training for low-income seniors. Helps gain skills for unsubsidized employment.[1][4]
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (20 hrs/wk) plus supportive services.

programs.name: Illinois Legal Assistance for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services for income-eligible older adults. Covers issues like benefits, housing, abuse prevention, and consumer problems.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors age 60+ with limited income; statewide legal aid network.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal representation; resolves issues protecting $10,000s in benefits.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints. Protects rights and improves quality of life in nursing homes and assisted living.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities in Illinois; advocacy for families.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy resolving 80%+ of complaints; statewide volunteer network.
